meta.purpose: Single source of truth for COHO methodology versions. Every score the site publishes carries one of these version stamps so a user who saved a ranking last month can see whether (and how) the formula has changed since.

opportunity_finder.label: Opportunity Finder score (0-100, by deal type)
opportunity_finder.what_it_covers: 5-dimension blended score for 482 Colorado places + counties. Re-tunes by deal type (9% competitive, 4% bond, preservation, workforce-resort, prop123-local, any).

change_log.what_changed: F236 added URA + Opportunity Zone overlap as civic-readiness signals. F241 added +10pt civic boost for housing-in-mission URA match.
F9 weight re-tune: civic 0.10 → 0.18 to better reward Prop 123 / housing-lead / comp-plan completeness. Recency 0.30 → 0.22 to balance.
F25/F26 added Private Activity Bond cap context to 4% deal scoring (informational; not a score input).
F13 widened default basis filter from 'both QCT+DDA' to 'either QCT or DDA'. Capture filter ON by default.
F8/F10 rebuild at jurisdiction level (482 places); per-deal-type weight tables; market-capture advantage added as informational column.
Initial county-level Opportunity Finder.

housing_needs_scorecard.label: Housing Needs Scorecard (0-100)
housing_needs_scorecard.what_it_covers: 4-component percentile-normalized scorecard for Colorado counties. Place-level inherits containing county.

change_log.what_changed: Switched from absolute thresholds to percentile-normalized ranking against all 64 CO counties. 4 components: blended cost burden, deep-need share, affordability pressure, worst-case need.
Initial absolute-threshold scorecard.

ami_gap.label: AMI-tier income vs. rent gap
ami_gap.what_it_covers: For each AMI tier (30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, 100%): number of renter households at or below tier minus number of rental units priced affordably for that tier.

change_log.what_changed: F28-1 fixed CHAS area→household weighting bug that under-counted small towns.
Expanded from 5 AMI bands to 7 (added 70% and 100% tiers for workforce + middle-income segmentation).
Initial 5-band AMI gap.

market_capture_advantage.label: Market capture advantage (rent vs LIHTC ceiling)
market_capture_advantage.what_it_covers: Per-county: HUD Fair Market Rent 2BR − LIHTC 60% AMI 2BR ceiling. Positive = LIHTC rent leaves room; negative = LIHTC ceiling above market (deal won't pencil at 60% AMI).

change_log.what_changed: P2 documentation of HUD FMR vs CHFA methodology. P6 use CHFA §42 rent in Deal Calc.
Initial capture-advantage calculation; ZORI cross-check added for tooltip context.

pma_site_score.label: Primary Market Analysis Site Score (0-100)
pma_site_score.what_it_covers: 5-dimension site-level feasibility score for a candidate parcel: demand (30%), capture risk (25%), rent pressure (15%), land/supply (15%), workforce (15%).

change_log.what_changed: Audit/Tier-1+2 fixes: surfaced severe burden, poverty, unemployment in the demand component.
Initial PMA Site Score.

deal_calculator.label: Deal Calculator pro forma
deal_calculator.what_it_covers: End-to-end sources + uses for a LIHTC deal: rents, NOI, debt sizing at 1.20 DSCR, eligible basis × applicable fraction × credit rate × 10 yr × equity price, gap, deferred fee.

change_log.what_changed: F239 wired Freddie Mac perm-rate benchmark (~5.90% implied). F247 corrected equity-price defaults — $0.87 → $0.86 national / $0.82 rural / $0.87 resort.
F230 Novogradac equity-price benchmark added as input default.
Q5 achievable-rent cap toggle for 70/80% AMI.
G/H/I/L6 multi-tranche soft debt, deferred fee balancing, 30-yr pro forma + replacement reserves, Year-15 exit analysis.
Initial Deal Calculator.

pab_allocation.label: Private Activity Bond local allocation
pab_allocation.what_it_covers: Per-jurisdiction PAB direct allocation + CHFA statewide pool context. Source: Colorado DOLA Division of Housing annual table.

change_log.what_changed: F26 reframed display so the CHFA pool is the headline and local allocations show as context (rare binding constraint for 4% deals).
F25 initial DOLA PAB allocation import.
